ref: So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said,
text: Yei nyinaa siiɛ sɛdeɛ asɛm a Awurade nam diyifoɔ he so kaeɛ he bɛbam sɛ,
translated: All this happened so that what the Lord had said through the prophet would be fulfilled:

ref: So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet Jeremiah had said,
text: Na diyifoɔ Yeremia asɛm he baam sɛ:
translated: The word of the prophet Jeremiah came as follows:

ref: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph while he was still in Egypt.
text: Na Herode wuiɛ he, hwɛ, Awurade bɔfoɔ yii ne ho adi daeɛ mu kyerɛɛ Yosef Misraim na ɔka kyerɛɛ no sɛ,
translated: After Herod died, behold, an angel of the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt and said to him,

ref: But when he heard that Herod's son Archelaus was now ruler of Judea, he was afraid to go there. Then in a dream he was told to go to Galilee,
text: Nso ɔtee sɛ Arkelao asi ne papa Herode anane mu oodi adeɛ Yudea he, osuroo sɛ ɔbɛkɔ hɔ. Na afei, bɛbɔɔ no kɔkɔ daeɛ mu he, ɔkɔɔ Galilea mansinim.
translated: But when he heard that Archelaus was reigning in Judea in place of his father Herod, he was afraid to go there. Having been warned in a dream, he withdrew to the district of Galilee.

ref: He said, “Turn back to God! The kingdom of heaven will soon be here.”
text: “Honsakra ho adwene, na soro ahennie he abɛne.”
translated: "If the mind is pure, the kingdom of heaven is near."

ref: When Jesus heard that John had been put in prison, he went to Galilee.
text: Na afei Yesu tee sɛ bɛakye Yohane he, osiim kɔɔ Galilea.
translated: After Jesus heard that John had been arrested, he went to Galilee.

ref: After Jesus had gone without eating for
text: Odii mmuada adaduannan, awia ne anadwo, na yei akyi he kɔm dee no.
translated: After forty days and nights, both day and night, then comes real hunger.

ref: The earth is God's footstool, so don't swear by the earth. Jerusalem is the city of the great king, so don't swear by it.
text: saa nso ne mfa asaase nka ntam, efiri sɛ ɔyɛ ne nane ntiasoɔ; mfa Yerusalem nso nka ntam, efiri sɛ ɔyɛ Hene kɛseɛ he ahenkuro.
translated: And do not swear by the earth, because it is his footstool; and do not swear by Jerusalem, because it is the city of the great King.

ref: You have been taught that a man who divorces his wife must write out divorce papers for her.
text: “Afei nso, bɛkaa sɛ, ‘Biaa a obegyae ne yerɛ he, ɔmma no awaregyaeɛ krataa.’
translated: And then, it will be said, ‘Whoever wants to leave his wife should give her a divorce letter.’

ref: You know our ancestors were told, “Don't use the Lord's name to make a promise unless you are going to keep it.”
text: “Bio, hoate sɛ bɛka kyerɛɛ tetefoɔ he sɛ, ‘Nka ntanhunu, nso fa wo ntankadeɛ ma Awurade.’
translated: Well, it is said that the elders used to say, 'Do not boast, but give your strength to the Lord.'

ref: But I tell you to love your enemies and pray for anyone who mistreats you.
text: Na me deɛ meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ, Honnɔ ho atamfoɔ na hommɔ mpaeɛ mma bɛ a bɛtaa ho,
translated: As for me, what I am saying is that, pray against the enemies of Honnɔ so that they do not prevail.

ref: When you do good deeds, don't try to show off. If you do, you won't get a reward from your Father in heaven.
text: “Honhwɛ yie na hoannyɔ ho tenenee nnipa anim sɛ bebehu ho; sɛ hoyɔ saa a hoonnya akatua ho Agya a ɔya soro he hɔ.
translated: Be careful not to do good deeds just to be seen by people; if you do so, you will have no reward from your Father who is in heaven.

ref: When you give to the poor, don't let anyone know about it.
text: Na sɛ wooyɔ adɔeɛ a, mma wo nsa benkum nnhu deɛ wo nsa nifa ɔɔyɔ,
translated: If you do a good deed, do not let your left hand know what your right hand is doing.

ref: Don't be like them. Your Father knows what you need even before you ask.
text: Na ho deɛ honnyɔ sɛ bɛ, efiri sɛ ho Agya nim deɛ ohia ho ansa na hoobisa no.
translated: It is not good to be proud, because the owner knows what is lacking before you ask him.

ref: But if you don't forgive others, your Father will not forgive your sins. Worshiping God by Going without Eating
text: nso sɛ hoammfa afoforɔ bɛ mfomsoɔ ankyɛ bɛ a, ho Agya a ɔya soro he nso ɔmmfa ho mfomsoɔ nkyɛ ho. Mmuadadie ho nkyerɛkyerɛ
translated: If you do not forgive others their faults, your Father in heaven will not forgive you your faults. Lesson on forgiveness

ref: Instead, comb your hair and wash your face.
text: Nso sɛ woodi mmuada a, sra wo tim, na hohoro w’anim,
translated: If you are going to a festival, wash your body and clean your face.

ref: I tell you not to worry about your life. Don't worry about having something to eat, drink, or wear. Isn't life more than food or clothing?
text: Nti meeka akyerɛ ho, honnwennwene ho kra ho, deɛ hobedi anaa deɛ hobɛnom, anaa ho nipadua ho, ɔfa deɛ hobɛhyɛ ho. Nkwa nsom bo nkyɛne aduane, na nipadua nkyɛne ntoma anaa?
translated: If I tell you about it, whether it is about food or drink, or about the body, it involves what you will put on it. Life does not value food more, and the body is not more valuable than clothing, is it?

ref: Ask, and you will receive. Search, and you will find. Knock, and the door will be opened for you.
text: Hommisa, na bɛde bɛma ho; honhwehwɛ, na hobehu; hommom, na bebebie pono he ama ho.
translated: Ask, and it will be given to you; seek, and you will find; knock, and the door will be opened for you.

ref: On the day of judgment many will call me their Lord. They will say, “We preached in your name, and in your name we forced out demons and worked many miracles.”
text: Saa da he, bebree bebisa me sɛ, Awurade, Awurade, yɛamfa wo dini anhyɛ nkɔm, na yɛamfa wo dini antutu ahonhommɔne, na yɛamfa wo dini annyɔ tumideɛ pii anaa?
translated: On that day, many will say to me, Lord, Lord, did we not prophesy in your name, and in your name drive out demons, and in your name perform many miracles?

ref: He took her by the hand, and the fever left her. Then she got up and served Jesus a meal.
text: Yesu sɔɔ ne nsa, na abunu he gyaeɛ, na ɔsɔreeɛ, na ɔyɔɔ no ahɔhoyɛ.
translated: Jesus stretched out his hand, touched the leper, and healed him.

ref: so the disciples went over to him and woke him up. They said, “Lord, wake up! Save us before we drown!”
text: Na asuafoɔ he baa ne nkyɛne bɛyanee no, sɛ, “Awurade, gye yɛ! Yɛɛhwere yɛ nkwa!”
translated: But the disciples went to him and woke him up, saying, "Lord, save us! We are perishing!"

ref: The men in the boat were amazed and said, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him.” Two Men with Demons in Them (Mark
text: Na yei yɛɛ bɛ nwanwa, na bɛkaa sɛ, “Abɛ nipa di a, mframa ne po mpo yɔ setie ma no yi?” Yesu tutuu ahonhommɔne ya Gadarene (Marko
translated: They were amazed and said, “What kind of man is this, that even the wind and the sea obey him?” Jesus cast out demons at Gadara (Mark)

ref: But after the crowd had been sent out of the house, Jesus went to the girl's bedside. He took her by the hand and helped her up.
text: Na bɛmaa nnipadɔm he kɔɔ abɔntene he, ɔkɔɔ dane he mu na ɔsɔɔ ne nsa, na abaayaa he sɔreeɛ.
translated: The man went to the crowd outside, he went into the room and raised his hand, and the woman stood up.

ref: When he saw the crowds, he felt sorry for them. They were confused and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
text: Berɛ a ohuu nnipadɔm he, ohuu bɛ mmɔbɔ, efiri sɛ na bɛɛteetee a na bɛabrɛ nso, na begu hɔ sɛ nwane a benni hwɛfoɔ.
translated: When he saw the crowds, he had compassion on them, because they were harassed and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.

ref: I promise you the day of judgment will be easier for the towns of Sodom and Gomorrah than for that town. Warning about Trouble (Mark
text: Nɔkorɛ meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ, atemuda he Nyankopɔn behu Sodom ne Gomora mpo mmɔbɔ akyɛne saa kuro he. Taa ho nkɔmhyɛ (Marko
translated: Truly I say concerning this, on the day of judgment God will show more mercy to Sodom and Gomorrah than to this town. This is a prophecy (Mark)

ref: When people mistreat you in one town, hurry to another one. I promise you before you have gone to all the towns of Israel, the Son of Man will come.
text: Sɛ bɛtaa ho kuro baako so a, honwane nkɔ kuro foforɔ so; na nɔkorɛ meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ, honnkɔ Israel nkuro nyinaa mu na Nipa Ba he aba.
translated: If you always stay in one town, you will not know about another town; and truly I tell you, do not go through all the towns of Israel before the Son of Man comes.

ref: Don't be afraid of people. They can kill you, but they cannot harm your soul. Instead, you should fear God who can destroy both your body and your soul in hell.
text: Honnsuro bɛ a beku honam nso bentumi nku kra; na mmom honsuro deɛ obetumi asɛe honhom ne honam bronsam gya mu.
translated: Do not be afraid of those who kill the body but cannot kill the soul; but rather be afraid of the one who can destroy both soul and body in hell fire.

ref: Even the hairs on your head are counted.
text: Na ho tinwii mpo bɛakane ne nyinaa.
translated: Even if it is small, it can do everything.

ref: Don't think I came to bring peace to the earth! I came to bring trouble, not peace.
text: “Honnwenne sɛ mede asomdwie ne baa asaase so; mammfa asomdwie amma, na mmom mede sekane.
translated: "Do not think that I have brought peace to the earth; I did not bring peace, but rather a sword."

ref: If you try to save your life, you will lose it. But if you give it up for me, you will surely find it. Rewards (Mark
text: Deɛ ne nsa aka ne kra he, obefiri ne nsa, na bɛ a me nti bɛbɛhwere bɛ kra he bɛ nsa bɛka. Akatua
translated: The one whose hand touches his soul, he will leave his hand, and because of me, he will wander, his soul will go where his hand touches. Reward.

ref: John the Baptist did not go around eating and drinking, and you said, “That man has a demon in him!”
text: Na Yohane baeɛ, na onnidi, na ɔnnom nso, na bɛkaa sɛ, ‘Ɔwɔ honhommɔne’;
translated: It was John, he did not eat, nor did he drink, and they said, ‘He has a demon’;

ref: But the Son of Man goes around eating and drinking, and you say, “That man eats and drinks too much! He is even a friend of tax collectors and sinners.” Yet Wisdom is shown to be right by what it does. The Unbelieving Towns (Luke
text: Nipa Ba he baeɛ a odidi na ɔnom, na hoka sɛ, “Ɔyɛ didifoɔ ne nsanomfoɔ, togyefoɔ ne nnebɔnyɛfoɔ bɛ adamfo!’ Nso, bɛ a bɛde Nyankopɔn nyansa di dwuma he da no adi sɛ ne nyansa he yɛ papa.” Nkuro a bɛansakra bɛ adwene he nnue (Luka
translated: The Son of Man came eating and drinking, and you say, “He is a glutton and a drunkard, a friend of tax collectors and sinners!” But wisdom is proved right by all her children. People who do not change their minds do not benefit (Luke)

ref: So God's promise came true, just as Isaiah the prophet had said,
text: Yei maa deɛ diyifoɔ Yesaia kaeɛ he baam sɛ:
translated: This is what the prophet Isaiah said about him:

ref: On that day they will be told they are either innocent or guilty because of the things they have said. A Sign from Heaven (Mark
text: efiri sɛ ho nsɛm so ne bɛnam bebu ho bem, na ho nsɛm nso so ne bɛnam bebu ho fɔ.” Bebisaa nsɛnkyerɛnne firii Yesu hɔ
translated: Because matters are judged by their evidence, and matters are also condemned by their evidence." They asked for a sign from Jesus.

ref: He was in the stomach of a big fish for three days and nights, just as the Son of Man will be deep in the earth for three days and nights.
text: Na sɛnea Yona dii bonsu yam nnansa, awia ne anadwo he, saa ara ne Nipa Ba he bedi nnansa asaase bunu mu.
translated: Just as Jonah was in the belly of the big fish for three days and three nights, so will the Son of Man be in the heart of the earth for three days.

ref: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked the plants.
text: Na aba he bi nso koguu nkasɛe mu, na nkasɛe he nyiniiɛ he omiaa aba he.
translated: Some seeds also fell among thorns, and the thorns grew up and choked them.

ref: Many prophets and good people were eager to see what you see and to hear what you hear. But I tell you they did not see or hear. Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Mark
text: Nɔkorɛ meeka akyerɛ ho, adiyifoɔ ne ateneneefoɔ bebree pɛɛ sɛ behu deɛ hoahu yi, nso bɛanhu, na bɛte deɛ hoate yi, nso bɛante. Mfantoho he asekyerɛ
translated: Truly I tell you, many prophets and righteous people longed to see what you see but did not see it, and to hear what you hear but did not hear it.

ref: But when Peter saw how strong the wind was, he was afraid and started sinking. “Save me, Lord!” he shouted.
text: Nso ohuu mframa dendene he, osuroeɛ, na ofirii aseɛ sɛ ɔɔmmem he, ɔteaam sɛ, “Awurade, gye me!”
translated: When he saw the strong wind, he became afraid, and as he began to sink, he shouted, "Lord, save me!"

ref: Jesus asked his disciples to bring the food to him,
text: Na ɔkaa sɛ, “Homfa mmra me.”
translated: And he/she said, "Bring them to me."

ref: Then he went up on a mountain where he could be alone and pray. Later in the evening, he was still there.
text: Na ogyaa bɛ kwane wieeɛ he, ɔkɔɔ bepɔ he so kɔbɔɔ mpaeɛ. Nnwunu dwoeɛ he, na ɔno nkoaa ne ya hɔ.
translated: When the fire finished clearing the path, he went to the top of the hill to pray. The cold was intense there, and he was the only one present.

ref: They begged him just to let them touch his clothes, and everyone who did was healed.
text: na bɛsrɛɛ no sɛ ɔma bɛmfa bɛ nsa nka n’ataadeɛ mmuano ano; na bɛ a bɛde bɛ nsa kaeɛ nyinaa nyaa ayaresa koraa.
translated: He begged him to let him touch the hem of his garment; and when he touched it, he was completely healed.

ref: But you let people get by without helping their parents when they should. You let them say that what they have has been offered to God.
text: Nso hoka sɛ deɛ ɔka kyerɛ ne papa anaa ne maame sɛ, ‘Deɛ nka wobenya afiri me hɔ yɛ akyɛdeɛ a yɛde ma Nyankopɔn a onhia sɛ saa nipa he di ne papa ne maame nni.’
translated: But if someone says to their father or mother, ‘What you would have received from me is a gift dedicated to God and so you cannot have it,’

ref: Jesus did not say a word. But the woman kept following along and shouting, so his disciples came up and asked him to send her away.
text: Nso wammua no koraa. Na n’asuafoɔ he baa ne nkyɛne bɛsrɛɛ no sɛ, “Pam no ma no nkɔ, na ɔɔteateam gu yɛ so dodo.”
translated: He did not answer at all. But his disciples came to him and begged him, saying, "Send her away, for she keeps crying out after us."

ref: Peter replied, “What did you mean when you talked about the things that make people unclean?”
text: Petro tee yei he, ɔka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Kyerɛ yɛ saa bɛ yi ase.”
translated: Peter was there, he said to him, "Explain the meaning of this proverb."

ref: Large crowds came and brought many people who were paralyzed or blind or lame or unable to talk. They placed them, and many others, in front of Jesus, and he healed them all.
text: Nnipadɔm pii de mpakye, mmubuafoɔ, anifirafoɔ, mmum, ne afoforɔ bebree kaa bɛ ho baa ne nkyɛne. Bɛde bɛ beguu ne nane ase, na ɔsaa bɛ yadeɛ,
translated: Many people with leprosy, the blind, the lame, the deaf, and many others came to him for salt. He placed the salt under his feet, and he healed diseases with the salt.

ref: Everyone was amazed at what they saw and heard. People who had never spoken could now speak. The lame were healed, the paralyzed could walk, and the blind were able to see. Everyone was praising the God of Israel. Jesus Feeds Four Thousand (Mark
text: na ɔyɛɛ nnipadɔm he nwanwa berɛ a behuu nea na ɔyɛ mmum he sɛ ɔɔkasa, bɛ a na bɛyɛ mpakye ne mmubuafoɔ sɛ bɛnnante, na nea na ɔyɛ anifirani he huu sɛ afie deɛ ohu adeɛ. Na bɛhyɛɛ Israel Nyankopɔn he animuonyam. Yesu maa nnipa mpemnan aduane (Marko
translated: When he saw the large crowd, he was amazed because he saw that they were like sheep without a shepherd, and he began to teach them many things. Those who were blind saw as if they were in a house seeing things. And he gave glory to the God of Israel. Jesus fed five thousand people (Mark)

ref: His disciples said, “This place is like a desert. Where can we find enough food to feed such a crowd?”
text: Na asuafoɔ he ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “He fa ne yebenya paanoo a ɔbɛso nnipa bebree yi serɛ so ha?”
translated: His disciples asked him, "Where can we get bread to feed all these people here?"

ref: and said, “Lord, have pity on my son! He has a bad case of epilepsy and often falls into a fire or into water.
text: kaa sɛ, “Awurade, hu me ba yi mmɔbɔ, na ɔyare twerɛ, na oohu amane; na ɔtaa tɔ gya ne nsuo mu.
translated: He said, "Lord, have mercy on my son, for he is epileptic and suffers greatly; he often falls into fire and water."

ref: Then Jesus spoke sternly to the demon. It went out of the boy, and right then he was healed.
text: Na Yesu teatea honhommɔne he, na ofirii ne mu, na abɔfra he nyaa ayaresa saa berɛ heaa.
translated: When Jesus had spoken these words, he came out, and the child was healed at that very moment.

ref: The king felt sorry for him and let him go free. He even told the official that he did not have to pay back the money.
text: Na akoa he wura he nyaa tema maa no, na ogyaa no de ne ka he kyɛɛ no.
translated: He took some gold and gave it to him, and he gave him his share as well.

ref: The king was so angry that he ordered the official to be tortured until he could pay back everything he owed.
text: Na ne wura he de abufuo de no hyɛɛ ahohiahiafoɔ nsa, kosi sɛ obetua ne ka a ɔde no nyinaa.
translated: It was his master who, out of anger, put the burden on the poor until he would pay all that he owed.

ref: Jesus answered, “Don't you know in the beginning the Creator made a man and a woman?
text: Obuaa sɛ, “Honkenkane sɛ, mfitiaseɛ he, beema ne baa ne Nyankopɔn bɔɔ bɛ
translated: He said, “Remember that, in the beginning, man and woman were created by God.”

ref: Jesus replied, “You certainly will drink from my cup! But it isn't for me to say who will sit at my right side and at my left. This is for my Father to say.”
text: Na ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Kuruwaa a mɛnom he deɛ ho nso hobɛnom, nso sɛ hobɛtena me nsa nifa ne benkum so deɛ, ɔnnyɛ me ne mede bɛma, na mmom, ɔya hɔ ma bɛ a m’ Agya asiesie de ama bɛ.”
translated: And he said to them, “The cup that I will drink, you will also drink, but to sit at my right and left hand is not for me to give, but it is for those for whom it has been prepared by my Father.”

ref: The man then told his younger son to go work in the vineyard. The boy said he would, but he didn't go.
text: Papa he kɔɔ deɛ ɔtɔ so mmienu he hɔ kɔkaa asɛm korɔ heaa kyerɛɛ no; na ne ba he buaa sɛ, ‘Owura mɛkɔ’; nso woankɔ.
translated: The father went to the one who is second in line and said the same thing to him; and his son answered, ‘Sir, I will go’; but he did not go.

ref: The chief priests and leaders answered, “He will kill them in some horrible way. Then he will rent out his vineyard to people who will give him his share of grapes at harvest time.”
text: Bɛka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Ɔbɛsɛe saa nnipa bɔnefoɔ he pasaa, na ɔde bobe turo he ama nhwɛsofoɔ foforɔ a bɛde afudeɛ bɛbra no twaberɛ.”
translated: He told him, “He will completely destroy those wicked people, and he will lease the vineyard to other tenants who will give him his share of the crop at harvest time.”

ref: Teacher, Moses wrote that if a married man dies and has no children, his brother should marry the widow. Their first son would then be thought of as the son of the dead brother.
text: “Kyerɛkyerɛfoɔ, Mose kaa sɛ, ‘Sɛ beema bi ware na wanwo ba na owu a, ne nua nware kunafoɔ he, na wawo mma ama ne nua a waawu he.’
translated: Teacher, Moses said, ‘If a man marries and dies without having children, his brother should marry the widow and have children for his deceased brother.’

ref: Go out to the street corners and tell everyone you meet to come to the banquet.”
text: Nti honkɔ mmɔntene so, na homfrɛ biaa a hobehu no ma no mmra ayefohyia apontoɔ he ase.’
translated: Do not walk on the street alone, and whenever you see someone, invite them to the meeting place at the entrance.

ref: The second most important commandment is like this one. And it is, “Love others as much as you love yourself.”
text: Na deɛ ɔtɔ so mmienu a ɔte sɛ ɔno ne sɛ: ‘Dɔ wo yɔnko sɛ wo ho.’
translated: But the second one, which is like it, is: ‘Love your neighbor as yourself.’

ref: Jesus said to the crowds and to his disciples:
text: Afei Yesu ka kyerɛɛ nnipadɔm he ne n’asuafoɔ he sɛ,
translated: Then Jesus said to the crowd and his disciples that,

ref: The Pharisees and the teachers of the Law are experts in the Law of Moses.
text: “Atwerɛfoɔ ne Farisifoɔ he atena Mose akonnwa so;
translated: The scribes and the Pharisees sit in Moses’ seat;

ref: You blind fools! Which is greater, the gold or the temple that makes the gold sacred?
text: Ho anifirafoɔ ne nkwaseafoɔ! Deɛ ɔya he ne sombo, sikakɔkɔɔ he anaa hyiadane he a woama sikakɔkɔɔ he ho ate he?
translated: Look at the fools and the stupid people! Who is better, the red gold or the silver that you have made the red gold look like?

ref: Are you blind? Which is more important, the gift or the altar that makes the gift sacred?
text: Ho ani afira paa! Na deɛ ɔya he ne sombo, akyɛdeɛ he anaa afɔrebokyia a ɔma akyɛdeɛ he ho te he?
translated: But look at this! Who is it that brings a gift, and where is the altar on which he places the gift?

ref: You won't see me again until you say, “Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord.”
text: Na meeka akyerɛ ho, honhu me bio de bɛkɔ akosi sɛ hobɛka sɛ, ‘Nhyira ne deɛ ɔɔba Awurade dini mu he.’ ”
translated: Let me show you, see me again until you will say, ‘Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord.’

ref: You will be arrested, punished, and even killed. Because of me, you will be hated by people of all nations.
text: “Saa mmerɛ he bebeyi ho ama sɛ bɛmfa homfa ahohiahia mu, na bɛaku ho, na me dini nti amane nyinaa bɛtane ho.
translated: At that time, it will be difficult so that you will go through suffering, and you will endure it, and because of my name all troubles will come upon you.

ref: If you are out in the field, don't go back for your coat.
text: deɛ ɔya afuom nsane mma fie mmɛfa ataadeɛ.
translated: The one who goes to the farm should not return home to take clothes.

ref: No one knows the day or hour. The angels in heaven don't know, and the Son himself doesn't know. Only the Father knows.
text: “Nso saa da ne dɔnhwere he deɛ biaa nnim, soro abɔfoɔ, anaa Ɔba he mpo nnim, gye sɛ Agya he nkoaa.
translated: No one knows that day or hour, not even the angels in heaven, nor the Son, but only the Father.

ref: You may be sure a servant who is always faithful will be put in charge of everything the master owns.
text: Nɔkorɛ meeka akyerɛ ho, ɔbɛma saa akoa he adi n’agyapadeɛ nyinaa so.

ref: He took her by the hand, and the fever left her. Then she got up and served Jesus a meal.
text: Yesu sɔɔ ne nsa, na abunu he gyaeɛ, na ɔsɔreeɛ, na ɔyɔɔ no ahɔhoyɛ.
translated: Jesus stretched out his hand, touched him, and healed him.

ref: A teacher of the Law of Moses came up to him and said, “Teacher, I'll go anywhere with you!”
text: Twerɛfoɔ bi baa ne nkyɛne sɛ, “Kyerɛkyerɛni, medi w’akyi baabi a wobɛkɔ biaa.”
translated: One scribe came to him and said, “Teacher, I will follow you wherever you go.”

ref: so the disciples went over to him and woke him up. They said, “Lord, wake up! Save us before we drown!”
text: Na asuafoɔ he baa ne nkyɛne bɛyanee no, sɛ, “Awurade, gye yɛ! Yɛɛhwere yɛ nkwa!”
translated: But the disciples went to him and woke him up, saying, "Lord, save us! We are perishing!"

ref: The men in the boat were amazed and said, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him.” Two Men with Demons in Them (Mark
text: Na yei yɛɛ bɛ nwanwa, na bɛkaa sɛ, “Abɛ nipa di a, mframa ne po mpo yɔ setie ma no yi?” Yesu tutuu ahonhommɔne ya Gadarene (Marko
translated: They were amazed and asked, “Who is this man, that even the wind and the sea obey him?” Jesus cast out demons at Gadara (Mark)

ref: After Jesus had crossed the lake, he came to shore near the town of Gadara and started down the road. Two men with demons in them came to him from the tombs. They were so fierce that no one could travel that way.
text: Na Yesu duruu asuogya hɔ he, Gadarenefoɔ asaase so he, nnipa mmienu a bɛwɔ honhommɔne firi afunsieɛ behyiaa no. Na bɛ ho hu nti, na nnipa biaa ntumi ntwam saa kwane he so.
translated: When Jesus arrived at the other side, in the region of the Gadarenes, two men possessed by demons came out from the tombs to meet him. Because they were so violent, no one could pass that way.

ref: Some teachers of the Law of Moses said to themselves, “Jesus must think he is God!”
text: Na atwerɛfoɔ bi keka kyerɛɛ bɛ ho bɛ ho sɛ, “Beema yi ɔɔka abususɛm.”
translated: Some writers have said about the proverb that, "This man spoke in parables."

ref: But after the crowd had been sent out of the house, Jesus went to the girl's bedside. He took her by the hand and helped her up.
text: Na bɛmaa nnipadɔm he kɔɔ abɔntene he, ɔkɔɔ dane he mu na ɔsɔɔ ne nsa, na abaayaa he sɔreeɛ.
translated: The man went to the crowd outside, he went into the room and raised his hand, and the woman stood up.

ref: When he saw the crowds, he felt sorry for them. They were confused and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
text: Berɛ a ohuu nnipadɔm he, ohuu bɛ mmɔbɔ, efiri sɛ na bɛɛteetee a na bɛabrɛ nso, na begu hɔ sɛ nwane a benni hwɛfoɔ.
translated: When he saw the crowd, he had compassion on them, because they were harassed and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.

ref: I promise you the day of judgment will be easier for the towns of Sodom and Gomorrah than for that town. Warning about Trouble (Mark
text: Nɔkorɛ meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ, atemuda he Nyankopɔn behu Sodom ne Gomora mpo mmɔbɔ akyɛne saa kuro he. Taa ho nkɔmhyɛ (Marko
translated: Truly I say to you, on the day of judgment God will show Sodom and Gomorrah more mercy than this town. This is a prophecy (Mark)

ref: Watch out for people who will take you to court and have you beaten in their synagogues.
text: Honhwɛ ho ho yie bɛ ho, efiri sɛ bɛde ho bɛma badwafoɔ na bɛahwe ho bɛ hyia adane mu;
translated: Take good care of yourself, because neglecting yourself will make you suffer and face difficulties.

ref: Because of me, you will be dragged before rulers and kings to tell them and the Gentiles about your faith.
text: na me nti bɛbɛtwe ho akɔ amradofoɔ ne ahemfo anim, na ayɛ adansedie ama bɛ ne Amanamane mufoɔ.
translated: Because of me, it will be brought before chiefs and kings, and it will be a testimony for them and for foreigners.

ref: When people mistreat you in one town, hurry to another one. I promise you before you have gone to all the towns of Israel, the Son of Man will come.
text: Sɛ bɛtaa ho kuro baako so a, honwane nkɔ kuro foforɔ so; na nɔkorɛ meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ, honnkɔ Israel nkuro nyinaa mu na Nipa Ba he aba.
translated: If you stay in one town for too long, move to another town; and truly I tell you, do not go through all the towns of Israel before the Son of Man comes.

ref: Don't be afraid of people. They can kill you, but they cannot harm your soul. Instead, you should fear God who can destroy both your body and your soul in hell.
text: Honnsuro bɛ a beku honam nso bentumi nku kra; na mmom honsuro deɛ obetumi asɛe honhom ne honam bronsam gya mu.
translated: Do not be afraid of those who kill the body but cannot kill the soul; but rather fear the one who can destroy both soul and body in hell fire.

ref: Aren't two sparrows sold for only a penny? But your Father knows when any one of them falls to the ground.
text: Bɛnntɔno mpatuprɛ mmienu nnye kaprɛ anaa? Nso bɛ mu baako mpo nkwati ho Agya ntɔ fam.
translated: Are two goats’ horns not better than a bushbuck’s? Yet, not even one among them touches the ground, Father.

ref: Even the hairs on your head are counted.
text: Na ho tinwii mpo bɛakane ne nyinaa.
translated: Even if it is small, it can say all its own.

ref: Don't think I came to bring peace to the earth! I came to bring trouble, not peace.
text: “Honnwenne sɛ mede asomdwie ne baa asaase so; mammfa asomdwie amma, na mmom mede sekane.
translated: I did not come to bring peace on earth; I did not bring peace, but rather a sword.

ref: If you try to save your life, you will lose it. But if you give it up for me, you will surely find it. Rewards (Mark
text: Deɛ ne nsa aka ne kra he, obefiri ne nsa, na bɛ a me nti bɛbɛhwere bɛ kra he bɛ nsa bɛka. Akatua
translated: The one whose hand has touched their soul, it will come from their hand, and because of me, it will get lost, the soul will go where the hand touches. Reward.

ref: Anyone who welcomes a prophet, just because that person is a prophet, will be given the same reward as a prophet. Anyone who welcomes a good person, just because that person is good, will be given the same reward as a good person.
text: Deɛ ɔgye diyifoɔ ya diyifoɔ dini mu he benya diyifoɔ akatua; na deɛ ɔgye nipa tenenee ya nipa tenenee dini mu he benya nipa tenee akatua;
translated: Whoever receives a prophet because he is a prophet will receive a prophet’s reward; and whoever receives a righteous person because he is righteous will receive a righteous person’s reward;

ref: John the Baptist did not go around eating and drinking, and you said, “That man has a demon in him!”
text: Na Yohane baeɛ, na onnidi, na ɔnnom nso, na bɛkaa sɛ, ‘Ɔwɔ honhommɔne’;
translated: It was John, and he did not eat, nor did he drink, and they said, ‘He has a demon’;

ref: But the Son of Man goes around eating and drinking, and you say, “That man eats and drinks too much! He is even a friend of tax collectors and sinners.” Yet Wisdom is shown to be right by what it does. The Unbelieving Towns (Luke
text: Nipa Ba he baeɛ a odidi na ɔnom, na hoka sɛ, “Ɔyɛ didifoɔ ne nsanomfoɔ, togyefoɔ ne nnebɔnyɛfoɔ bɛ adamfo!’ Nso, bɛ a bɛde Nyankopɔn nyansa di dwuma he da no adi sɛ ne nyansa he yɛ papa.” Nkuro a bɛansakra bɛ adwene he nnue (Luka
translated: The Son of Man came eating and drinking, and you say, “Here is a glutton and a drunkard, a friend of tax collectors and sinners!” But wisdom is proved right by all her children. The people who did not change their minds did not benefit (Luke)

ref: People of Capernaum, do you think you will be honored in heaven? You will go down to hell! If the miracles that took place in your town had happened in Sodom, it would still be standing.
text: Na wo Kapernaum nso, bɛbɛpegya wo akɔ soro anaa? Daabi da, bɛde wo bɛkɔ asamando. Efiri sɛ, bɛyɔɔ tumideɛ a bɛayɔ no wo mu he Sodom a, nka Sodom kuro he ya hɔ de besi nnɛ.
translated: And you, Capernaum, will you be lifted up to heaven? No, you will go down to the grave. For if the miracles that were performed in you had been done in Sodom, it would have remained to this day.

ref: If you are tired from carrying heavy burdens, come to me and I will give you rest.
text: Ho a hoabrɛ, na ho so nnesoa nyinaa, hommra me nkyɛne, na mɛma ho ahomee.
translated: Come to me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest.

ref: but warned them not to tell anyone about him.
text: na ɔhyɛɛ bɛ sɛ bɛmma biaa nhu ne nipa bane.
translated: He said that no man knows his enemy.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as Isaiah the prophet had said,
text: Yei maa deɛ diyifoɔ Yesaia kaeɛ he baam sɛ:
translated: This is what the prophet Isaiah said about him:

ref: “Here is my chosen servant! I love him, and he pleases me. I will give him my Spirit, and he will bring justice to the nations.
text: “Yei ne m’akoa a mayi no, medɔ ba, ɔno ne me kra ani sɔ no. Mehwie me Honhom agu ne so, na wapaem aka atɛntenenee akyerɛ Amanamane mufoɔ.
translated: This is my chosen servant, my beloved child, in whom my soul delights. I will pour my Spirit upon him, and he will proclaim justice to the nations.

ref: Some people brought to Jesus a man who was blind and could not talk because he had a demon in him. Jesus healed the man, and then he was able to talk and see.
text: Na bɛde bi a ɔwɔ honhommɔne a ɔyɛ nifirani na ɔsane yɛ mum braa no; na ɔsaa no yadeɛ, na deɛ na nka ɔyɛ mum he kasaeɛ na ohuu adeɛ.
translated: There was a certain man who was possessed by an evil spirit and was also mute; and he was healed, and the mute man spoke and saw.

ref: Jesus knew what they were thinking, so he said to them: Any kingdom where people fight each other will end up ruined. And a town or family that fights will soon destroy itself.
text: Na onim bɛ adwene, nti ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Ahennie biaa a ɔne ne ho di asie he bɛsɛe, na kuro anaa fie biaa a ɔko tia ne ho he, onnyina.
translated: If a kingdom is divided against itself, it will be ruined, and a town or house divided against itself cannot stand.

ref: When an evil spirit leaves a person, it travels through the desert, looking for a place to rest. But when the demon doesn't find a place,
text: “Sɛ honhommɔne tutu firi nipa mu kɔ a, okokyinikyini akwaweɛ so sɛ ɔɔpɛ homebea, na sɛ wannya bi a,
translated: When the soul leaves a person, it wanders restlessly as if searching for a resting place, and if it does not find one,

ref: On that day they will be told they are either innocent or guilty because of the things they have said. A Sign from Heaven (Mark
text: efiri sɛ ho nsɛm so ne bɛnam bebu ho bem, na ho nsɛm nso so ne bɛnam bebu ho fɔ.” Bebisaa nsɛnkyerɛnne firii Yesu hɔ
translated: Because matters are judged by their circumstances, and circumstances are judged by their faults.” He asked for a sign from Jesus.

ref: He was in the stomach of a big fish for three days and nights, just as the Son of Man will be deep in the earth for three days and nights.
text: Na sɛnea Yona dii bonsu yam nnansa, awia ne anadwo he, saa ara ne Nipa Ba he bedi nnansa asaase bunu mu.
translated: Just as Jonah was in the belly of the big fish for three days and three nights, so will the Son of Man be in the heart of the earth for three days.

ref: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked the plants.
text: Na aba he bi nso koguu nkasɛe mu, na nkasɛe he nyiniiɛ he omiaa aba he.
translated: If a seed falls into bad soil, it is the bad soil that causes the seed to perish.

ref: Many prophets and good people were eager to see what you see and to hear what you hear. But I tell you they did not see or hear. Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Mark
text: Nɔkorɛ meeka akyerɛ ho, adiyifoɔ ne ateneneefoɔ bebree pɛɛ sɛ behu deɛ hoahu yi, nso bɛanhu, na bɛte deɛ hoate yi, nso bɛante. Mfantoho he asekyerɛ
translated: Truly I tell you, many prophets and righteous people desired to see what you see, but did not see it, and to hear what you hear, but did not hear it. Parable explanation

ref: The kingdom of heaven is like what happens when someone finds a treasure hidden in a field and buries it again. Such a person is happy and goes and sells everything in order to buy that field. A Valuable Pearl
text: “Soro ahennie he te sɛ agyapadeɛ a bɛde asie asaase bi so, na bi huiɛ na ɔkata so; na ɔde ho anigyeɛ kɔtɔnoo deɛ ɔwɔ nyinaa kɔtɔɔ asaase he. Ahwene pa ho mfantoho
translated: Royalty is like a treasure hidden in a land, and someone finds it and covers it; and with joy he goes and sells all he has and buys that land. A good bead has no disguise.

ref: The kingdom of heaven is like what happens when a shop owner is looking for fine pearls.
text: “Bio, soro ahennie te sɛ dwadini bi a ɔɔhwehwɛ ahwene pa;
translated: Life, royal power is like a hunter who seeks good beads;

ref: and went to his hometown. He taught in their synagogue, and the people were so amazed that they asked, “Where does he get all this wisdom and the power to work these miracles?
text: Na afei Yesu baa ɔnoaa ne kurom, na ɔhyɛɛ aseɛ sɛ ɔɔkyerɛkyerɛ nkurofoɔ he bɛ hyiadane mu hɔ, na bɛ ho dwirii bɛ maa bɛkaa sɛ, “He ne saa nipa yi konyaa nyansa ne tumi nnwuma a ɔte sei?
translated: Then Jesus came to his hometown, and he began to teach the people in their synagogue, and they were amazed and said, “Where did this man get this wisdom and miraculous powers?”

ref: But when Peter saw how strong the wind was, he was afraid and started sinking. “Save me, Lord!” he shouted.
text: Nso ohuu mframa dendene he, osuroeɛ, na ofirii aseɛ sɛ ɔɔmmem he, ɔteaam sɛ, “Awurade, gye me!”
translated: When he saw the strong wind, he became afraid, and as he began to sink, he shouted, "Lord, save me!"

ref: Jesus asked his disciples to bring the food to him,
text: Na ɔkaa sɛ, “Homfa mmra me.”
translated: And he/she said, "Bring them to me."

ref: Then he went up on a mountain where he could be alone and pray. Later in the evening, he was still there.
text: Na ogyaa bɛ kwane wieeɛ he, ɔkɔɔ bepɔ he so kɔbɔɔ mpaeɛ. Nnwunu dwoeɛ he, na ɔno nkoaa ne ya hɔ.
translated: When the fire finished clearing the path, he went to the top of the hill to pray. The cold was intense there, and he was the only one present.

ref: They begged him just to let them touch his clothes, and everyone who did was healed.
text: na bɛsrɛɛ no sɛ ɔma bɛmfa bɛ nsa nka n’ataadeɛ mmuano ano; na bɛ a bɛde bɛ nsa kaeɛ nyinaa nyaa ayaresa koraa.
translated: He begged him to let him touch the hem of his garment; and when he touched it, he was completely healed.

ref: But you let people get by without helping their parents when they should. You let them say that what they have has been offered to God.
text: Nso hoka sɛ deɛ ɔka kyerɛ ne papa anaa ne maame sɛ, ‘Deɛ nka wobenya afiri me hɔ yɛ akyɛdeɛ a yɛde ma Nyankopɔn a onhia sɛ saa nipa he di ne papa ne maame nni.’
translated: But if someone says to his father or mother, ‘Whatever help you might otherwise have received from me is a gift devoted to God,’ then he does not have to honor his father or mother.

ref: Jesus did not say a word. But the woman kept following along and shouting, so his disciples came up and asked him to send her away.
text: Nso wammua no koraa. Na n’asuafoɔ he baa ne nkyɛne bɛsrɛɛ no sɛ, “Pam no ma no nkɔ, na ɔɔteateam gu yɛ so dodo.”
translated: He didn’t answer at all. But his disciples came to him and begged him, saying, “Send her away, for she keeps crying out after us.”

ref: Peter replied, “What did you mean when you talked about the things that make people unclean?”
text: Petro tee yei he, ɔka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Kyerɛ yɛ saa bɛ yi ase.”
translated: Peter came there, he told him, "Explain the meaning of this proverb."

ref: Large crowds came and brought many people who were paralyzed or blind or lame or unable to talk. They placed them, and many others, in front of Jesus, and he healed them all.
text: Nnipadɔm pii de mpakye, mmubuafoɔ, anifirafoɔ, mmum, ne afoforɔ bebree kaa bɛ ho baa ne nkyɛne. Bɛde bɛ beguu ne nane ase, na ɔsaa bɛ yadeɛ,
translated: Many people, including the lame, the blind, the crippled, the dumb, and many others, gathered around the drum. The drum was placed at his feet, and he healed the drum.

ref: Everyone was amazed at what they saw and heard. People who had never spoken could now speak. The lame were healed, the paralyzed could walk, and the blind were able to see. Everyone was praising the God of Israel. Jesus Feeds Four Thousand (Mark
text: na ɔyɛɛ nnipadɔm he nwanwa berɛ a behuu nea na ɔyɛ mmum he sɛ ɔɔkasa, bɛ a na bɛyɛ mpakye ne mmubuafoɔ sɛ bɛnnante, na nea na ɔyɛ anifirani he huu sɛ afie deɛ ohu adeɛ. Na bɛhyɛɛ Israel Nyankopɔn he animuonyam. Yesu maa nnipa mpemnan aduane (Marko
translated: when he was among the crowd it was amazing when he saw what he was doing among them as he spoke, it was as if he was among the lame and the dumb as they walked, and what he did among the blind made them see as if a house sees things. And he gave glory to the God of Israel. Jesus fed four thousand people (Mark)

ref: Jesus called his disciples together and told them, “I feel sorry for these people. They have been with me for three days, and they don't have anything to eat. I don't want to send them away hungry. They might faint on their way home.”
text: Na Yesu frɛɛ n’asuafoɔ he baa ne nkyɛne na ɔkaa sɛ, “Nnipadɔm yi yɛ me mmɔbɔ paa, efiri sɛ bɛatena me nkyɛne nnansa nso benni hwee a bebedi; na mempɛ sɛ mɛma bɛde kɔm akɔ, anyɛ saa a bebetumi atwa ato kwanem.”
translated: When Jesus called his disciples to him, he said, “I have great compassion for this crowd, because they have stayed with me for three days and have nothing to eat; and I do not want to send them away hungry, or they might collapse on the way.”

ref: His disciples said, “This place is like a desert. Where can we find enough food to feed such a crowd?”
text: Na asuafoɔ he ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “He fa ne yebenya paanoo a ɔbɛso nnipa bebree yi serɛ so ha?”
translated: His disciples asked him, “Where can we get bread here to feed all these people?”

ref: Jesus asked them how much food they had. They replied, “Seven small loaves of bread and a few little fish.”
text: Yesu bisaa bɛ sɛ, “Howɔ paanoo dodoɔ mmeɛhe?” Na bɛkaa sɛ, “Nson, ne nkyenam nketewa kakraa bi.”
translated: Jesus asked, “How many loaves of bread do you have?” And they replied, “Seven, and a few small fish.”

ref: Don't you know by now that I am not talking to you about bread? Watch out for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees!
text: Mmɛ nti ne ho nteaseɛ sɛ ɔnyɛ paanoo asɛm ne meeka? Honhwɛ yie Farisifoɔ ne Sadukifoɔ mmɔreka ho!”
translated: So does it mean he is not simply talking about bread? Watch out for the Pharisees and Sadducees’ teachings!

ref: Then Jesus asked, “But who do you say I am?”
text: Obisaa bɛ sɛ, “Ho nso hodɛ me ne hwane?”
translated: The visitor will say, “What about me and the sheep?”

ref: I will give you the keys to the kingdom of heaven, and God in heaven will allow whatever you allow on earth. But he will not allow anything you don't allow.
text: Mɛma wo soro ahennie he safoa, na deɛ wobɛkyekyere no asaase yi so he na bɛakyekyere no soro dada, na deɛ wobɛsane no asaase so he na bɛasane no soro dada.”
translated: “I will give you the key to the kingdom of heaven, and whatever you bind on this earth will be bound in heaven, and whatever you loose on this earth will be loosed in heaven.”

ref: Peter took Jesus aside and told him to stop talking like that. He said, “God would never let this happen to you, Lord!”
text: Na Petro de no gyinaa nkyɛne na ofirii aseɛ kaa n’anim sɛ, “Awurade! Yei mpare wo. Yei ntumi nto wo da!”
translated: But Peter did not wait and he began to speak out, saying, "Lord! This will never happen to you. This can never befall you!"

ref: and said, “Lord, have pity on my son! He has a bad case of epilepsy and often falls into a fire or into water.
text: kaa sɛ, “Awurade, hu me ba yi mmɔbɔ, na ɔyare twerɛ, na oohu amane; na ɔtaa tɔ gya ne nsuo mu.
translated: He said, "Lord, have mercy on my son, for he is epileptic and suffers greatly; he often falls into fire and into water."

ref: Then Jesus spoke sternly to the demon. It went out of the boy, and right then he was healed.
text: Na Yesu teatea honhommɔne he, na ofirii ne mu, na abɔfra he nyaa ayaresa saa berɛ heaa.
translated: When Jesus noticed their thoughts, he left there, and the child was healed at that very moment.

ref: Peter answered, “From foreigners.” Jesus replied, “Then their own people don't have to pay.
text: Na Petro kaa sɛ, “Bɛgye firi afoforɔ hɔ,” he, Yesu ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Saa deɛ a, na mma he de bɛ ho.”
translated: But Peter said, "Take it from others," then Jesus said to him, "If so, then the children are free."

ref: It will be terrible for people who cause even one of my little followers to sin. Those people would be better off thrown into the deepest part of the ocean with a heavy stone tied around their necks!
text: Sɛ ho mu bi to mmɔfra nketewa a bɛgye me di yi mu baako sintidua a, nsunsuansoɔ a ɔbɛba ne so he so kyɛne sɛ bɛde ayuyammoɔ bɛsɛne ne kɔno mu de no akɔto po bunu mu.
translated: If any harm befalls little children and you believe me in this matter, the consequences that will follow will be as bitter as using bitter leaves to rub on their mouths and then throwing them into the deep sea.

ref: The king felt sorry for him and let him go free. He even told the official that he did not have to pay back the money.
text: Na akoa he wura he nyaa tema maa no, na ogyaa no de ne ka he kyɛɛ no.
translated: He took some gold and gave it to him, and he gave him his share.

ref: The king was so angry that he ordered the official to be tortured until he could pay back everything he owed.
text: Na ne wura he de abufuo de no hyɛɛ ahohiahiafoɔ nsa, kosi sɛ obetua ne ka a ɔde no nyinaa.
translated: It is my master who, out of anger, put the suffering upon the poor, until he pays back all that he owes.

ref: Some Pharisees wanted to test Jesus. They came up to him and asked, “Is it right for a man to divorce his wife for just any reason?”
text: Na Farisifoɔ baa ne nkyɛne bɛsɔɔ no hwɛeɛ, sɛ, “Mmara ma ho kwane sɛ asɛm bi nti beema bi gyae ne yerɛ anaa?”
translated: Some Pharisees came and tested him, saying, "Is it lawful for a man to divorce his wife for any reason?"

ref: Jesus answered, “Don't you know in the beginning the Creator made a man and a woman?
text: Obuaa sɛ, “Honkenkane sɛ, mfitiaseɛ he, beema ne baa ne Nyankopɔn bɔɔ bɛ
translated: He said, "Remember that, in the beginning, man and woman were created by God."

ref: The Pharisees asked Jesus, “Why did Moses say a man could write out divorce papers and send his wife away?”
text: Afei bebisaa no sɛ, “Hedeɛ, mmɛ nti ne Mose hyɛɛ yɛ sɛ, bi betumi ama awaregyaeɛ krataa na wagyae baa he?”
translated: Then they asked him, “So why did Moses command that a man give his wife a certificate of divorce and send her away?”

ref: “Which ones?” the man asked. Jesus answered, “Do not murder. Be faithful in marriage. Do not steal. Do not tell lies about others.

text: Na bɛkataa boɔ he ano, de nsɔano hyɛɛ no nso maa awɛmfoɔ he wɛne Yesu damena he.
translated: They rolled a stone over the entrance, and the guards put a seal on it and watched over where Jesus was laid.

ref: Then Jesus said, “Don't be afraid! Tell my followers to go to Galilee. They will see me there.” Report of the Guard
text: Afei Yesu ka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Honnsuro; honkɔ nkɔbɔ me nuanom amaneɛ sɛ bɛnkɔ Galilea; na bebehu me hɔ.” Awɛmfoɔ he amanebɔ
translated: Then Jesus said to them, "Do not be afraid; go and tell my brothers to go to Galilee; they will see me there." The guards report the news.

ref: The guards shook from fear and fell down, as though they were dead.
text: Na ne ho hu nti, awɛmfoɔ he ho popoeɛ, na bɛyɛɛ sɛ nea bɛawuwu.
translated: Because of his fear, the shepherd ran away, and it was as if he would die.

ref: About that time Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee, and John baptized him in the Jordan River.
text: Saa nna he mu he, Yesu firi Nasaret a ɔya Galilea baeɛ, na Yohane bɔɔ no asu Yordan mu.
translated: At that time, Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee, and John baptized him in the Jordan.

ref: Jesus felt sorry for the man. So he put his hand on him and said, “I want to! Now you are well.”
text: Na Yesu nyaa tema maa no, na ɔtenee ne nsa kaa no, na ɔka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Mepɛ, wo ho mfiri.”
translated: When Jesus had compassion on him, he reached out his hand and touched him, and said to him, "I am willing, be clean."

ref: As soon as Jesus left the synagogue with James and John, they went home with Simon and Andrew.
text: Na befirii hyiadane he muaa pɛ, bɛ ne Yakobo ne Yohane kɔɔ Simon ne Andreo fie.
translated: When they left the synagogue, they went with James and John to the home of Simon and Andrew.

ref: When they got there, Jesus was told that Simon's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.
text: Na Simon asebaa yare abunu da hɔ. Na ntɛmaa bɛkaa ne ho asɛm kyerɛɛ Yesu.
translated: Simon’s mother-in-law was sick and lying down. And people went and told Jesus about her.

ref: In fact, the whole town gathered around the door of the house.
text: Na kuro mũ he nyinaa behyiaa pono he ano.
translated: When I left, we all met at the door.

ref: Very early the next morning before daylight, Jesus got up and went to a place where he could be alone and pray.
text: Anɔpa a animu nteteeɛ he, Yesu sɔre kɔhyɛɛ kokoam baabi, na ɔbɔɔ mpaeɛ hɔ.
translated: Very early in the morning, Jesus got up and went to a solitary place, and there he prayed.

ref: “Why would he say such a thing? He must think he is God! Only God can forgive sins.”
text: “Ɛɛne nti ne ɔkasa saa? Ɔyɛ abususɛm! Hwane ne tumi de bɔne kyɛ ka Nyankopɔn ho?”
translated: So why does he speak like that? He is disrespectful! Who uses their power to associate evil with God?

ref: Jesus answered: The friends of a bridegroom don't go without eating while he is still with them.
text: Yesu bisaa bɛ sɛ, “Nnipa a bɛkɔ ayefohyia ntumi nkyene kɔm berɛ a ayefokunu he ya bɛ nkyɛne, ɔnte saa’? Mmerɛ tentene a ayefokunu he ne bɛya hɔ he, ɔnsɛ sɛ bɛkyene kɔm.
translated: Jesus asked, “Can the guests of the bridegroom fast while the bridegroom is with them? As long as they have the bridegroom with them, they should not fast.”

ref: Some Pharisees asked Jesus, “Why are your disciples picking grain on the Sabbath? They are not supposed to do that!”
text: Yei maa Farisifoɔ he bisaa no sɛ, “Hwɛ, mmɛ nti ne bɛɛyɔ deɛ otia mmara homeda?”
translated: Then some Pharisees asked him, “Look, why are they doing what is unlawful on the Sabbath?”

ref: Jesus decided to ask some of his disciples to go up on a mountain with him, and they went.
text: Yesu kɔɔ bepɔ he so, na ɔtoo ne nsa frɛɛ bɛ a ohia bɛ ma bɛbaa ne nkyɛne.
translated: Jesus went up the mountain, and he raised his hand and called those who needed help to come to him.

ref: Some teachers of the Law of Moses came from Jerusalem and said, “This man is under the power of Beelzebul, the ruler of demons! He is even forcing out demons with the help of Beelzebul.”
text: Na atwerɛfoɔ he a befiri Yerusalem baeɛ he kaa sɛ, “Ɔwɔ Beelsebul, na ɔde ahonhommɔne panyini he tumidie ne tutu ahonhom fi.”
translated: Some scribes who came down from Jerusalem said, "He has Beelzebul, and by the ruler of demons he casts out demons."

ref: I promise you that any of the sinful things you say or do can be forgiven, no matter how terrible those things are.
text: Nɔkorɛ, meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ, ɔde nipa bɔne ne abususɛm biaa a ɔbɛka bɛkyɛ no,
translated: Truly, I will say that, for any evil person and any wrongdoing he will commit,

ref: Anyone who obeys God is my brother or sister or mother.”
text: Nea ɔyɔ Nyankopɔn apɛdeɛ biaa yɛ me nua beema ne me nua baa ne me maame.”
translated: Whoever does the will of God is my brother and my sister and my mother.

ref: The next time Jesus taught beside Lake Galilee, a big crowd gathered. It was so large that he had to sit in a boat out on the lake, while the people stood on the shore.
text: Na afei nso, Yesu hyɛɛ aseɛ maa nkyerɛkyerɛ po he ho. Nnipadɔm betwaa ne ho hyiaeɛ ma ɔkɔforoo kodoɔ bi po he so na ɔtenaa mu, berɛ a na nnipadɔm he tete po he ano.
translated: After that, Jesus began to teach by the lakeside. A crowd gathered around him so much that he got into a boat on the lake and sat in it, while all the people were along the shore at the water’s edge.

ref: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked out the plants. So they did not produce any grain.
text: Na aba he bi koguu nkasɛem, na nkasɛe he nyiniiɛ he, omiaa no, na anso aba biaa.
translated: There was a certain person who went to plant words, and as the words grew, he weeded them, but no fruit came at all.

ref: But they don't have roots, and they don't last very long. As soon as life gets hard or the message gets them in trouble, they give up.
text: Nso ɔnam sɛ benni nhini nti, begyina kakra bi, na sɛ haw anaa taa ba bɛ so ɔnam asɛm he nti a, bɛhwe ase ntɛm so.
translated: Because fish does not have scales, it spoils quickly, and if there is any problem or difficulty, it will go bad even faster.

ref: It is like what happens when a mustard seed is planted in the ground. It is the smallest seed in all the world.
text: Ɔte sɛ sinapi aba a sɛ bedua ne asaase mu a, ɔno ne aba a osua paa asaase so aba nyinaa mu;
translated: It is like a mustard seed which, when planted in the ground, is the smallest of all the seeds on earth;

ref: Jesus was in the back of the boat with his head on a pillow, and he was asleep. His disciples woke him and said, “Teacher, don't you care that we're about to drown?”
text: Nso na Yesu ada summiiɛ so ya kodoɔ he mu; na benyanee no bisaa no sɛ, “Kyerɛkyerɛfoɔ, yɛɛyera yi, ɔmfa wo ho?”
translated: While Jesus was lying down asleep in the boat, his disciples came and woke him up, saying, "Teacher, in this trouble, don't you care about us?"

ref: Jesus got up and ordered the wind and the waves to be quiet. The wind stopped, and everything was calm.
text: Onyaneeɛ, na ɔkaa mframa he anim ka kyerɛɛ po he sɛ, “Gyae, yɛ dinn!” Na mframa he ano brɛɛ ase, na ɔyɛɛ dinn.
translated: Then he spoke to the wind and told the sea, "Stop, be still!" And the wind calmed down, and it became quiet.

ref: Jesus asked his disciples, “Why were you afraid? Don't you have any faith?”
text: Obisaa bɛ sɛ, “Ɛɛne nti ne hosuro? Nti obesi nnɛ yi honni gyidie?”
translated: He will ask, “So why is he afraid? So will he not have faith today?”

ref: Then Jesus went inside and said to them, “Why are you crying and carrying on like this? The child isn't dead. She is just asleep.”
text: Na owuraa fie hɔ he, obisaa bɛ sɛ, “Mmɛ nti ne hooyɔ gyegyeegye na hoosu yi? Akwadaa he nwuiɛ, na waada.”
translated: When he got to the master's house, he asked, “Why is there so much wailing and crying here? Which child has died, and where have they been laid?”

ref: When they came to Jesus, they saw the man who had once been full of demons. He was sitting there with his clothes on and in his right mind, and they were terrified.
text: Na bɛbaa Yesu hɔ, na bebehuu nea kane he na ɔwɔ ahonhommɔne a na bɛfrɛ no Dɔm he sɛ ɔte hɔ fira ntoma na n’adwene mu ate. Na besuroeɛ.
translated: When Jesus arrived there, a man who had an evil spirit and was called Legion because he wore clothes and his mind was disturbed met him. And he was afraid.

ref: But the people laughed at him. After Jesus had sent them all out of the house, he took the girl's father and mother and his three disciples and went to where she was.
text: Na bɛseree no. Na ɔmaa bɛ nyinaa kɔɔ abɔntene, na ɔfaa akwadaa he papa ne ne maame ne bɛ a bɛka ne ho he, na ɔkɔɔ baabi a akwadaa he ya he.
translated: It was dawn. And all the sheep went outside, and he took the child with his father and mother and the sheep that stayed with him, and he went to where the child was sick.

ref: Once again Jesus got into the boat and crossed Lake Galilee. Then as he stood on the shore, a large crowd gathered around him.
text: Na afei berɛ a Yesu tware po he bio sane ba n’agya he, nnipadɔm kɛseɛ twaa ne ho hyiaeɛ, na ɔya po he ho.
translated: After Jesus crossed the sea again and returned to his father, a large crowd gathered around him, and he was by the sea.

ref: The woman had heard about Jesus, so she came up behind him in the crowd and barely touched his clothes.
text: Na waate Yesu ho asɛm, na ofirii n’akyi nnipadɔm he mu bɛkaa n’ataadeɛ ano,
translated: When she heard about Jesus, she came up behind him in the crowd and touched his garment,

ref: But Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone what had happened. Then he said, “Give her something to eat.”
text: Na ɔhyɛɛ bɛ ketee sɛ bɛmma biaa nte yei, na afei ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, bɛmma akwadaa he bibi nni.
translated: He made a short proverb saying no man is like this, and then he told the proverb that no boy has anything.

ref: If any place won't welcome you or listen to your message, leave and shake the dust from your feet as a warning to them.”
text: Baabi a hobɛkɔ a bɛannye ho he, na bɛantie ho he, sɛ hoofiri hɔ a, homporoporo ho nane ase mfuturo ngu hɔ, na ɔnyɛ adanseɛ ntia bɛ.”
translated: Where you are going that they did not accept it, and did not listen to it, when you leave there, shake the dust off your feet there, as a testimony against them.

ref: Herodias had a grudge against John and wanted to kill him. But she could not do it
text: Ne saa nti na Herodia wɔ meneasepɔ tia Yohane, na ɔpɛɛ sɛ oku no, nso wantumi,
translated: Herodias held a grudge against John and wanted to kill him, but she could not.

ref: because Herod was afraid of John and protected him. He knew that John was a good and holy man. Even though Herod was confused by what John said, he was glad to listen to him. And he often did.
text: efiri sɛ, na Herode suro Yohane, esiane sɛ onim sɛ ɔyɛ teneneeni ne kronkronni, nti ɔbɔɔ ne ho bane. Sɛ otie no a, na waayɛ basaa nso na ɔpɛ sɛ otie no.
translated: Because Herod was afraid of John, knowing that he was a righteous and holy man, he protected him. When he listened to him, he was greatly puzzled, yet he liked to listen to him.

ref: The girl left and asked her mother, “What do you think I should ask for?” Her mother answered, “The head of John the Baptist!”
text: Abaayaa he firii adi, na obisaa ne maame sɛ, “Ɛɛne ne memmisa?” Obuaa no sɛ, “Ka sɛ wopɛ Suboni Yohane ti.”
translated: One day, a child asked his mother, “What is my request?” She replied, “Say you want John the Baptist’s head.”

ref: When John's followers learned that he had been killed, they took his body and put it in a tomb. Jesus Feeds Five Thousand (Matthew
text: Na Yohane asuafoɔ he tee asɛm he, bɛba bɛfaa n’amu he na bɛkɔpɛɛ no koraberɛ bodane bi mu. Yesu maa Nnipa Mpem Nnum Aduane
translated: When John’s disciples heard the news, they came and took his body and laid it in a tomb. Jesus fed five thousand people.

ref: But so many people were coming and going that Jesus and the apostles did not even have a chance to eat. Then Jesus said, “Let's go to a place where we can be alone and get some rest.”
text: Ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Hommra baabi a biaa nni mmɛhome kakra,” efiri sɛ na nnipa bebree oodi akɔ ne aba a na ɔmma benya kwane nnidi mpo.
translated: He said to them, “Come to a place where there is at least a little rest,” because many people were coming and going and they did not even have a chance to eat.

ref: They sat down in groups of
text: Na bɛtenaa akuo akuo, ha ha ne aduonum aduonum.
translated: They sat in groups, thirty by thirty.

ref: Jesus' disciples picked up twelve large baskets of leftover bread and fish.
text: Na bɛsesaa paanoo ne nkyenam mproprowa nkɛntɛne du mmienu ma ma.
translated: He/she changed the bread and sardine for twelve tubers of yam for me.

ref: He could see that the disciples were struggling hard, because they were rowing against the wind. Not long before morning, Jesus came toward them. He was walking on the water and was about to pass the boat.
text: Na ohuu sɛ mframa dendene ɔɔbɔ hyia bɛ na ama hare ayɛ dene ama bɛ he, ɔbaa bɛnkyɛne anɔpa tutuutu, na ɔɔnante po he so. Ɔyɔɔ n’adwene sɛ ɔbetwam bɛ ho.
translated: It happened that a strong wind was blowing and made the weather very cold, so early in the morning, a woman came near the river and walked along its bank. She thought to herself that she would cross over there.

ref: As soon as they got out of the boat, the people recognized Jesus.
text: Na befirii kodoɔ he mu sii fam he, nnipa huu no ntɛm so,
translated: When the leaf fell down there, people saw it immediately,

ref: So they ran all over that part of the country to bring their sick people to him on mats. They brought them each time they heard where he was.
text: na bɛyɔɔ ntɛm de ayarefoɔ firi mpɔtamu hɔ nyinaa totoo kɛtɛ so braa no baabi a bɛtee sɛ na ɔya biaa.
translated: They quickly carried the patients from all over the compound to the mat where the man was lying, because they heard he was in pain.

ref: You are good at rejecting God's commands so that you can follow your own teachings!
text: Afei ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Honam nyansakwane so po Nyankopɔn mmara sɛdeɛ ɔbɛyɛ a hobetumi adi ho atetesɛm so!
translated: Then he said to them, "The wisdom of the flesh cannot surpass God's law so that one can boast about it!"

ref: The Pharisees and teachers asked Jesus, “Why don't your disciples obey what our ancestors taught us to do? Why do they eat without washing their hands?”
text: Nti Farisifoɔ ne atwerɛfoɔ he bisaa no sɛ, “Mmɛ nti ne w’asuafoɔ nni mpanyimfoɔ atetesɛm he so yi, na bɛde nsa a ho agu fi ne didi yi?”
translated: Then the Pharisees and the scribes asked him, “Why do your disciples not follow the tradition of the elders, but eat with unwashed hands?”

ref: It is useless for you to worship me, when you teach rules made up by humans.”
text: bɛsom me kwa, bɛkyerɛkyerɛ nipa ahyɛdeɛ sɛ nkyerɛkyerɛ pa.’
translated: Worship me in vain, teaching human rules as doctrines.

ref: But you let people get by without helping their parents when they should. You let them say that what they own has been offered to God.
text: Nso hoka sɛ, sɛ bi ka kyerɛ n’agya anaa ne na sɛ, ‘Mfasoɔ a nka wobenya afiri me so nyinaa yɛ Korban’ a,
translated: But if someone says to his father or mother, ‘Any benefit you might have received from me is Corban’

ref: Jesus called the crowd together again and said, “Pay attention and try to understand what I mean.
text: Afei ɔfrɛɛ nnipadɔm he bio ka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Ho nyinaa hontie me na honte aseɛ:
translated: Then he called the crowd to him again and said, "Everyone listen to me and understand:
